concat
date_range)
Easterr
returnc
    If holiday falls on Saturday, use following Monday instead;
    if holiday falls on Sunday, use Monday instead
    
weekdayr
</usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/pandas/tseries/holiday.py
next_monday%
    For second holiday of two adjacent ones!
    If holiday falls on Saturday, use following Monday instead;
    if holiday falls on Sunday or Monday, use following Tuesday instead
    (because Monday is already taken by adjacent holiday on the day before)
    r
dowr
next_monday_or_tuesday1
    If holiday falls on Saturday or Sunday, use previous Friday instead.
    r
previous_friday@
    If holiday falls on Sunday, use day thereafter (Monday) instead.
    r
sunday_to_mondayK
    If holiday falls on Sunday or Saturday,
    use day thereafter (Monday) instead.
    Needed for holidays such as Christmas observation in Europe
    r
weekend_to_mondayT
    If holiday falls on Saturday, use day before (Friday) instead;
    if holiday falls on Sunday, use day thereafter (Monday) instead.
    r
nearest_workdaya
    returns next weekday used for observances
    r
days
next_workdaym
    returns previous weekday used for observances
    r
previous_workdayx
    returns previous workday after nearest workday
    )
before_nearest_workday
    returns next workday after nearest workday
    needed for Boxing day or multiple holidays in a series
    )
after_nearest_workday
HolidayzY
    Class that defines a holiday with start/end dates and rules
    for observance.
    Nc
_	|	d
        Parameters
        ----------
        name : str
            Name of the holiday , defaults to class name
        offset : array of pandas.tseries.offsets or
                class from pandas.tseries.offsets
            computes offset from date
        observance: function
            computes when holiday is given a pandas Timestamp
        days_of_week:
            provide a tuple of days e.g  (0,1,2,3,) for Monday Through Thursday
            Monday=0,..,Sunday=6
        Examples
        --------
        >>> from pandas.tseries.holiday import Holiday, nearest_workday
        >>> from dateutil.relativedelta import MO
        >>> USMemorialDay = Holiday(
        ...     "Memorial Day", month=5, day=31, offset=pd.DateOffset(weekday=MO(-1))
        ... )
        >>> USMemorialDay
        Holiday: Memorial Day (month=5, day=31, offset=<DateOffset: weekday=MO(-1)>)
        >>> USLaborDay = Holiday(
        ...     "Labor Day", month=9, day=1, offset=pd.DateOffset(weekday=MO(1))
        ... )
        >>> USLaborDay
        Holiday: Labor Day (month=9, day=1, offset=<DateOffset: weekday=MO(+1)>)
        >>> July3rd = Holiday("July 3rd", month=7, day=3)
        >>> July3rd
        Holiday: July 3rd (month=7, day=3, )
        >>> NewYears = Holiday(
        ...     "New Years Day", month=1,  day=1, observance=nearest_workday
        ... )
        >>> NewYears  # doctest: +SKIP
        Holiday: New Years Day (
            month=1, day=1, observance=<function nearest_workday at 0x66545e9bc440>
        )
        >>> July3rd = Holiday("July 3rd", month=7, day=3, days_of_week=(0, 1, 2, 3))
        >>> July3rd
        Holiday: July 3rd (month=7, day=3, )
        Nz&Cannot use both offset and observance.)
NotImplementedError

Holiday.__repr__Fc
        Calculate holidays observed between start date and end date
        Parameters
        ----------
        start_date : starting date, datetime-like, optional
        end_date : ending date, datetime-like, optional
        return_name : bool, optional, default=False
            If True, return a series that has dates and holiday names.
            False will only return dates.
        N)

filter_start_dateZ
filter_end_dater
datesZ
holiday_datesr
Holiday.datesc
        Get reference dates for the holiday.
        Return reference dates for the holiday also returning the year
        prior to the start_date and year following the end_date.  This ensures
        that any offsets to be applied will yield the holidays within
        the passed in dates.
        Nr
years)

endZ
freqrH

Holiday._reference_datesc
        Apply the given offset/observance to a DatetimeIndex of dates.
        Parameters
        ----------
        dates : DatetimeIndex
            Dates to apply the given offset/observance rule
        Returns
        -------
        Dates with rules applied
        Nc

AbstractHolidayCalendarzH
    Abstract interface to create holidays following certain rules.
    z
list[Holiday]
rulesi
        Initializes holiday object with a given set a rules.  Normally
        classes just have the rules defined within them.
        Parameters
        ----------
        name : str
            Name of the holiday calendar, defaults to class name
        rules : array of Holiday objects
            A set of rules used to create the holidays.
        N)
z AbstractHolidayCalendar.__init__c
ruler
rule_from_name
z&AbstractHolidayCalendar.rule_from_nameFc
        Returns a curve with holidays between start_date and end_date
        Parameters
        ----------
        start : starting date, datetime-like, optional
        end : ending date, datetime-like, optional
        return_name : bool, optional
            If True, return a series that has dates and holiday names.
            False will only return a DatetimeIndex of dates.
        Returns
        -------
            DatetimeIndex of holidays
        Nz
Holiday Calendar z" does not have any rules specifiedr
.0rr
<listcomp>
z4AbstractHolidayCalendar.holidays.<locals>.<listcomp>)
dtyper
	Exceptionr/
_cacher
objectZ
sort_indexrB
pre_holidays
holidaysr
z AbstractHolidayCalendar.holidaysc
        Merge holiday calendars together. The base calendar
        will take precedence to other. The merge will be done
        based on each holiday's name.
        Parameters
        ----------
        base : AbstractHolidayCalendar
          instance/subclass or array of Holiday objects
        other : AbstractHolidayCalendar
          instance/subclass or array of Holiday objects
        c
holidayr

z7AbstractHolidayCalendar.merge_class.<locals>.<dictcomp>c

other_holidaysZ
base_holidaysr
merge_class
z#AbstractHolidayCalendar.merge_classc
        Merge holiday calendars together.  The caller's class
        rules take precedence.  The merge will be done
        based on each holiday's name.
        Parameters
        ----------
        other : holiday calendar
        inplace : bool (default=False)
            If True set rule_table to holidays, else return array of Holidays
        N)

USFederalHolidayCalendarz
    US Federal Government Holiday Calendar based on rules specified by:
    https://www.opm.gov/policy-data-oversight/
       snow-dismissal-procedures/federal-holidays/
    z
